text: Deadlines are a common part of work and study, but handling them well is a skill anyone can learn. Good time management begins with breaking a large task into smaller steps and scheduling each one. Prioritization is also key—focus on the most urgent or important tasks first, rather than multitasking. Be realistic about what you can achieve; if a project seems too large, practice scope management by reducing less critical elements. Most importantly, communicate early if you foresee a problem. Letting colleagues or teachers know in advance builds trust and often leads to helpful solutions. With these habits, deadlines become less stressful and more manageable.
